name: Bosque El Tejedelo Trail
name_original: Bosque de Tejedelo — running circular por Requejo de Sanabria y parque natural Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores
description: Jog the moderate 3.0-mile Bosque El Tejedelo Trail through an ancient yew forest in Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores.
region_intro: Jogging around Requejo offers routes through the scenic province of Zamora, Castile and León, Spain. The region is characterized by the Duero River and its dramatic "Arribes" (steep river canyons), providing varied terrain for runners. Routes often feature views of the historic Requejo bridge, an iron arch structure spanning the Duero. The natural environment includes riverside paths and elevated viewpoints, suitable for combining exercise with appreciation for the landscape. Best jogging routes around Requejo The most popular jogging route is Bosque El Tejedelo Trail, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail that takes about 44 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads through a dense forest landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Chestnut Path and Hermisende Meadows — running circular through the Valley, a moderate 4.8 miles (7.7 km) path. This route offers views of meadows and a valley. Local runners also love the Sotillo Waterfall and Lake Trail, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail leading through areas with a waterfall and lake, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Jogging around Requejo is defined by the Duero River, steep river canyons, and historic bridges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Requejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 260 runners have used komoot to explore Requejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Requejo?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;9 dedicated running routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Requejo, offering a variety of experiences for different fitness levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there any easy jogging paths for beginners in Requejo?
A: While most routes around Requejo are moderate to difficult, the region's natural environment, characterized by the Duero River and its dramatic 'Arribes', offers varied terrain. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026614893/bosque-el-tejedelo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque El Tejedelo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.8 km) and leads through a dense forest.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Requejo area?
A: Yes, there are circular running routes available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026623991/chestnut-path-and-hermisende-meadows-running-circular-through-the-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chestnut Path and Hermisende Meadows — running circular through the Valley&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) path offers scenic views of meadows and a valley, perfect for a varied run.

Q: Are there any running trails that feature waterfalls or lakes?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026652248/sotillo-waterfall-and-lake-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall and Lake Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic option. This 7.8-mile (12.6 km) route leads through areas featuring the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4708534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and nearby lakes, providing stunning natural scenery for your run. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotillo Waterfall – Laguna de Sotillo loop from Cobreros&lt;/a&gt; for another challenging option.

Q: What kind of natural landmarks or points of interest can I see while running in Requejo?
A: Requejo is rich in natural beauty. Many routes offer views of the iconic Requejo bridge, an impressive iron arch structure spanning the Duero River within the stunning Arribes del Duero area. You can also discover highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Serrada Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Lake Sanabria&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the region's dramatic river canyons and landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Requejo offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Sanabria loop from Ribadelago Viejo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.5-mile (16.9 km) route with significant elevation gain, perfect for those seeking a demanding run with rewarding views.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Requejo?
A: The running routes in Requejo are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the scenic beauty of the Duero River and its 'Arribes', and the inspiring views of the historic Requejo bridge.

Q: Is Requejo suitable for trail running with wildlife spotting opportunities?
A: The natural environment of Requejo, particularly within the Arribes del Duero area, provides a rich habitat. While specific wildlife sightings are not guaranteed, running through dense forests like those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026614893/bosque-el-tejedelo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque El Tejedelo Trail&lt;/a&gt; or along riverside paths increases your chances of encountering local flora and fauna.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Requejo?
A: The region's climate, typical of Castile and León, generally offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures and lush landscapes, making them ideal for exploring the running routes along the Duero River and through the 'Arribes del Duero'.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of Lake Sanabria?
A: Yes, some routes and nearby highlights provide stunning views of Lake Sanabria. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20148581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Sanabria loop from Ribadelago Viejo&lt;/a&gt; offers direct views of the lake. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Lake Sanabria&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that can be incorporated into longer runs or visited separately for its panoramic vistas.

Q: Are the running trails in Requejo suitable for families?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't provided for all routes, the region offers varied terrain. For families with older children or those accustomed to moderate activity, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026614893/bosque-el-tejedelo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque El Tejedelo Trail&lt;/a&gt; (3.0 miles, moderate) could be enjoyable, allowing them to experience the forest landscape together.
tips: In the Arroyo Tejedelo Recreational Area we will find two wooden tables and benches to recharge energy and continue with our route. It is located in an unbeatable place, in a beautiful setting near a wooden bridge over the Tejedelo stream.

Following the route along an ascending path, we will arrive at this beautiful viewpoint, from which we can see the wonderful panorama of Tejedelo, a mixed forest dominated by oaks and holly where the dark crowns of the yew trees stand out.
Following the path marked with the beacons, we enter the forest of "El Tejedelo" where we will be accompanied by majestic ancient yew trees, which appear like giants on the sides of the path.

faqs.question: Is the Bosque El Tejedelo Trail suitable for jogging?
faqs.answer: While komoot classifies this route as 'jogging', the trail is primarily known as a hiking route with a 'moderate' difficulty. It features diverse terrain, including stony paths, rocky slopes, and ascents with 'strong slopes'. The estimated walking time for the 4.7 km route is about 4 hours, suggesting it's not ideal for continuous, fast-paced jogging. It's better suited for a challenging hike or a very slow, technical jog for experienced trail runners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter sections with low scrub and oak woods, stony paths, and considerable rocky slopes that can be wet or icy. There are also wooden walkways designed to protect the delicate forest floor and guide you. The route crosses streams multiple times, adding to its diverse character.

faqs.question: What are the main attractions or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary attraction is the ancient yew forest itself, known as the Tejedelo Forest, home to over a hundred millennial yew trees, some over 1,500 years old. You can also enjoy panoramic views from the Peñas del Veladero Viewpoint, where the dark canopies of the yews stand out. The Arroyo Tejedelo Recreation Area is another notable spot.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Bosque El Tejedelo Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Requejo de Sanabria. While specific parking details aren't provided, visitors typically park in or near the village of Requejo de Sanabria to access the trailhead. Look for designated parking areas upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The research does not explicitly state rules regarding dogs on the Bosque El Tejedelo Trail. However, as it's located within the Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife and delicate ecosystem. Always practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of specific permits or entrance fees required for the Bosque El Tejedelo Trail itself. Similarly, access to the Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores, where the trail is located, typically does not require an entrance fee. However, always check local regulations before your visit, especially for any updated rules regarding protected natural areas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Bosque El Tejedelo Trail?
faqs.answer: The ancient yew forest creates a unique microclimate, being a few degrees cooler in summer and warmer in winter. This makes it a pleasant visit during most seasons. However, given the rocky and potentially wet/icy sections, spring and autumn might offer the most comfortable conditions, avoiding extreme temperatures and reducing the risk of slippery terrain. The vibrant biodiversity is also particularly beautiful during these seasons.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The humid environment and rich biodiversity of the yew forest support a variety of wildlife. Keep an eye out for roe deer, wild boars, martens, and even wildcats. The forest is a sanctuary for many species, so moving quietly increases your chances of spotting them.

faqs.question: Is the Bosque El Tejedelo Trail a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the komoot route for the Bosque El Tejedelo Trail is a circular route, meaning it forms a loop and brings you back to your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Requejo de Sanabria. While there are no facilities directly on the trail, you can find cafes and restaurants in the village of Requejo de Sanabria for refreshments before or after your visit.

faqs.question: What are the general regulations for visiting the Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores?
faqs.answer: As the trail is within the Parque Natural del Lago de Sanabria y Alrededores, visitors should adhere to general natural park regulations. This typically includes staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife or plants, taking all trash with you, and respecting the natural environment. Always check the official park website or visitor centers for the most current and specific rules.
